converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Photograph album of Siam
Thomson, John
4863
98 items. 98 items.
1865-1866 Albumen prints, individually matted. Album photographs of Siam (Thailand). Subjects include Siamese royalty, religious figures and residences, politicos, group social scenes, theatre folk, street scenes, musicians, and other views of Bangkok and Thailand. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 11 ======= 11 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2297674
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
February 1994
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, May 2004
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Album photographs of Siam (Thailand). Subjects include Siamese royalty, religious figures and residences, politicos, group social scenes, theatre folk, street scenes, musicians, and other views of Bangkok and Thailand.
RELATED MATERIALS
Item list available.
Cite As:
Photograph Album of Siam, #4863. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Thailand -- Religious life and customs. Thailand -- Social life and customs. Thailand -- 20th century. Thailand -- Kings and rulers. Thailand -- Description and travel. Thailand -- Pictorial works. Albumen prints.
2297674 <<<<<<< HEAD Prince George, eldest Son of the second King11Like the preceding, but in undress12A younger brother of Prince George, a Midshipman in the Siamese Navy13Mum Rachadie, Chief of the Foreign Police14The Superior of a Siamese Wat15The celebrated image of Budda, of green Jasper, considered by the Siamese their Palladium in Wat Baromaniwat16View of the Temples, Bell-tower, etc., of Wat Tepatida, Bangkok17View of the Temple Buildings of Wat Ratshmada, Bangkok18The out-buildings of a Wat19The Wat Saket Monastery, Bangkok. Temples of Wat Po in the distance110Priest's Residences at the Wat Saket Monastery or Temple, Bangkok21The Hankatong at Wat Saket22Library of the Wat Saket Temple or Monastery, Bangkok23Not Identified24Bird's eye view of the buildings of a Wat. View over the city from Wat Saket25The Kankatong at Wat Saket26Illegible27View of Wat B, from one of the islets in the river Island of the Maks Nak by Nat Saket28Court of Wat Dokami (Wat Boudet ?)29A group of Siamese men and boys210A Lahhon (?) with recitals31A Siamese woman in her usual dress32A Siamese woman, added to this is a Siamese lady - sister of the King's wives33A group of Siamese women34A Siamese Priest reading a Homily35Musicians36Musicians37Laos reciting a love-story, accompanied by the clapping of the hands of the rest and the sound of the Laospipe38Female musicians39Siamese playing a single stick or quarter-staff310Lenn Champann, a Siamese play41Ipong (?) A play with Cowries (?)42Lenn Tuah, a game played for money43Thott Caett, played upon the same principle as the foregoing44Siamese boxing45Women sowing (sic)46Cleaning and spinning cotton47Purifying and refining gold48A Siamese female shampooed by her servants49Dressing the queue410Shampooing by female medical attendant51Children, the mother lying before the fire52Cutting hair53A Siamese Noble being conveyed in a hammock to a visit54A visitor, the lady's servants in attendance55A Siamese Official dictating a Despatch (sic)56Cleaning (gleaming ?) rice from the husk57Breaking the husk58A Siamese at dinner, his wives preparing59A Siamese Noble going on a visit510A man carrying water, a woman and girl offering fruits and vegetables for sale61War junks on the Samsen Creek62H. Brit, Majesty's Consulate, The Consul's residence63Not identified, but probably the Consul's residence64Precincts of a Wat65In Cambodia66The Mah'a nak Canal, suburbs of Bangkok, Siam67View of Bangkok, taken from the Landing Stage (?) of H.M. Consulate68Her Brit, Majesty's Consulate, The Council's Interpreter and first and second Assistant's residences69The Great Swing (missing as of Sept. 11, 2000)610Pra Rahu, riding Krat, holding a mallet, a Brahminical deity to whom offerings are made in Siam when evils are to be averted or success sought71Pra Narai, four-armed riding Krut younger brother of Pra In Suan72Pra Ankan, riding a buffalo, holding an oblong shiled and sword. The Planet Mars deified. A Brahinical divinity, invoked in Siam to avert evil73Pra Ket, riding a serpent and holding a sabre. A Brahminical deity to whom offerings are made in Siam, when evils are to be averted and success sought74Pra Sau, riding a tiger and holding a trident. A Brahminical divinity (a deification of the planet Saturn) whose aid the Siamese invoke to avert evil, etc.75Pra Hat Bodi, riding a deer and holding a long-handled lance. The planet Jupiter personified, a Brahminical deity, invoked in Siam to avert evil, etc.76Pra I Suan, riding an ox and holding a trident (the siva of the Brahmins)77Pra Tsan, riding a horse, holding a circular shield and sword. The moon god. A Brahminical deity invoked in Siam to avert misfortune, etc.78Pra Atit, riding Traison, holding a serpent-shaped bow. A Brahminical deity (the Sun deified) invoked by the Siamese to avert evil and ensure success79Pra Suk, riding an ox and holding a snake-shaped bow. The planet Venus deified. A Brahminical divinity, invoked in Siam to avert evil, etc.710Pra Plon holding a sword, riding a rhinoceros, a Brahminical deity, invoked by the Siamese81Pra Prom, with four faces and eight arms, riding the bird called Hong. A deity to the Bramins to whom offerings are made, sometimes, in Siam82Pramaha Wikinet (Gunesha of the Hindus) a son of Pra Insuan, held in great esteem by Siamese physicians83Pra Put, riding an elephant and holding a sword in each hand. A Brahminical deity (the planet Mercury personified) invoked to avert calamity, etc.84Siamese play - actors85Siamese actor, the Executioner of the Sentence86Siamese play - actors87Figures from a Wat (?) referring to religious traditions88Siamese play - actors89A Siamese actor, the Execution(er ?) in the Tragedy810An actor, representing a Prince91One of the Maids of the Queen, fancy dress of H.M. the King92Siamese play - actors93Siamese actors94Siamese play - actors95Siamese play - actors96Siamese play - actors97Siamese play - actors (? not identified)98Siamese actors in rehersal99Siamese actors, representing a Princess910Siamese play - actors101Siamese actors102Siamese actors - two Princesses with conical headdresses103A Siamese actor, a young lady representing a Prince104Wat Shaang (?) or Cheng105Not Identified106Siamese play - actors107 ======= Prince George, eldest Son of the second King11Like the preceding, but in undress12A younger brother of Prince George, a Midshipman in the Siamese Navy13Mum Rachadie, Chief of the Foreign Police14The Superior of a Siamese Wat15The celebrated image of Budda, of green Jasper, considered by the Siamese their Palladium in Wat Baromaniwat16View of the Temples, Bell-tower, etc., of Wat Tepatida, Bangkok17View of the Temple Buildings of Wat Ratshmada, Bangkok18The out-buildings of a Wat19The Wat Saket Monastery, Bangkok.
